steps.title: Find the opening comma that signals a parenthetical phrase
steps.content:
The sentence contains: subject “The arrangement of the figures,” → phrase “according to Swentzell” → verb “represents...” The comma before “according to” indicates this phrase is being set apart as extra information.

Removing it confirms the core is complete: “The arrangement of the figures represents her idea.”

steps.title: Apply the enclosing-comma rule
steps.content: A comma pairs with the opening comma to enclose the parenthetical phrase between subject and verb. A colon introduces elaborations rather than enclosing; a dash without pairing is non-standard; no punctuation orphans the opening. That matches choice B — B is correct.
